children.heading: Port and Intermodal Improvement Program.—
children.label: a Port and Intermodal Improvement Program.—

children.heading: .—General authority
children.label: 1 .—General authority
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary of Transportation shall make grants, on a competitive basis, to eligible applicants to assist in funding eligible projects for the purpose of improving the safety, efficiency, or reliability of the movement of goods through ports and intermodal connections to ports.

children.label: A
children.text: A State.

children.label: B
children.text: A political subdivision of a State, or a local government.

children.label: C
children.text: A public agency or publicly chartered authority established by 1 or more States.

children.label: D
children.text: A special purpose district with a transportation function.

children.label: E
children.text: An Indian Tribe (as defined in section 4 of the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act (), without regard to capitalization), or a consortium of Indian Tribes.25 U.S.C. 5304

children.label: F
children.text: A multistate or multijurisdictional group of entities described in this paragraph.

children.label: G
children.text: A lead entity described in subparagraph (A), (B), (C), (D), (E), or (F) jointly with a private entity or group of private entities, including the owners or operators of a facility, or collection of facilities at a port.

children.label: I
children.text: within the boundary of a port; or

children.label: II
children.text: outside the boundary of a port, but is directly related to port operations or to an intermodal connection to a port; and

children.label: I
children.text: the loading and unloading of goods at the port, such as for marine terminal equipment;

children.label: II
children.text: the movement of goods into, out of, around, or within a port, such as for highway or rail infrastructure, intermodal facilities, freight intelligent transportation systems, and digital infrastructure systems;

children.label: III
children.text: operational improvements, including projects to improve port resilience;

children.label: aa
children.text: port electrification or electrification master planning;

children.label: bb
children.text: harbor craft or equipment replacements or retrofits;

children.label: cc
children.text: development of port or terminal microgrids;

children.label: dd
children.text: providing idling reduction infrastructure;

children.label: ee
children.text: purchase of cargo handling equipment and related infrastructure;

children.label: ff
children.text: worker training to support electrification technology;

children.label: gg
children.text: installation of port bunkering facilities from oceangoing vessels for fuels;

children.label: hh
children.text: electric vehicle charge or hydrogen refueling infrastructure for drayage and medium or heavy duty trucks and locomotives that service the port and related grid upgrades; or

children.label: ii
children.text: other related port activities, including charging infrastructure, electric rubber-tired gantry cranes, and anti-idling technologies; or

children.label: V
children.text: port and port-related infrastructure that supports seafood and seafood-related businesses, including the loading and unloading of commercially harvested fish and fish products, seafood processing, cold storage, and other related infrastructure.

children.label: B
children.text: notwithstanding paragraph (6)(A)(v), to provide financial assistance to 1 or more projects under subparagraph (A) for development phase activities, including planning, feasibility analysis, revenue forecasting, environmental review, permitting, and preliminary engineering and design work.

children.label: i
children.text: is necessary for a project described in paragraph (3)(A)(ii)(III) of this subsection; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is not receiving assistance under chapter 537; or

children.label: B
children.text: for any project within a small shipyard (as defined in section 54101).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: To be eligible for a grant under this subsection or subsection (b), an eligible applicant shall submit to the Secretary an application in such form, at such time, and containing such information as the Secretary considers appropriate.

children.label: I
children.text: certify the facility for which a covered applicant is applying for a grant has an approved facility security plan pursuant to that addresses the cybersecurity risks of such digital infrastructure or software component; orsection 70103(c) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: if the approved facility security plan of a facility for which a covered applicant is applying for a grant does not address such cybersecurity risks, provide a brief description in the application of how such applicant will address the cybersecurity risks of such digital infrastructure or software component.

children.heading: .—Update of facility security plan
children.label: iii .—Update of facility security plan
children.text: If the approved facility security plan required under of a facility for which a covered applicant is applying for a grant under this subsection does not address the cybersecurity risks of digital infrastructure or a software component to be acquired by such grant and such applicant provides a brief description to address such cybersecurity risks under clause (ii)(II), the covered applicant shall ensure that such security plan is updated to address the cybersecurity risks described in clause (ii)(II) in the next update required under paragraph (3)(G) of such section.section 70103(c) of this title

children.heading: .—Covered applicant defined
children.label: iv .—Covered applicant defined
children.text: In this paragraph, the term “covered applicant” means an applicant under this subsection that is not otherwise eligible under subsection (b).

children.heading: .—Solicitation process
children.label: B .—Solicitation process
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the date that amounts are made available for grants under this subsection or subsection (b) for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall solicit grant applications for eligible projects in accordance with this subsection.

children.heading: .—Delayed notice of funding opportunity
children.label: C .—Delayed notice of funding opportunity
children.text: If the Secretary amends a published solicitation for grant applications such that an applicant would need the information contained in the amendment to draft an application, other than an amendment of the amount of grant funding available, the Secretary shall extend the application deadline by the number of days between the initial solicitation and the amendment.

children.label: i
children.text: the project improves the safety, efficiency, or reliability of the movement of goods through a port or intermodal connection to a port;

children.label: ii
children.text: the project is cost effective (except in the case of a project described under subparagraph (D));

children.label: iii
children.text: the eligible applicant has authority to carry out the project;

children.label: iv
children.text: the eligible applicant has sufficient funding available to meet the matching requirements under paragraph (8);

children.label: v
children.text: the project will be completed without unreasonable delay; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the project cannot be easily and efficiently completed without Federal funding or financial assistance available to the project sponsor.

children.label: i
children.text: the utilization of non-Federal contributions;

children.label: ii
children.text: the net benefits of the funds awarded under this subsection, considering the cost-benefit analysis of the project, as applicable (except in the case of a project described under subparagraph (D)); and

children.label: iii
children.text: a port’s increased resilience as a result of the project.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: C .—In general
children.text: In selecting projects described in paragraph (3), the Maritime Administrator, in consultation with the Secretary of Defense, may give priority to providing funding to strategic seaports in support of national security requirements.

children.heading: .—Noncontiguous states and territories
children.label: D .—Noncontiguous states and territories
children.text: The requirements under subparagraphs (A)(ii) and (B)(ii) shall not apply in the case of a project described in paragraph (3) in a noncontiguous State or territory.

children.heading: .—Geographic distribution
children.label: A .—Geographic distribution
children.text: Not more than 25 percent of the amounts made available for grants under this subsection for a fiscal year may be used to make grants for projects in any 1 State.

children.heading: .—Small projects
children.label: B .—Small projects
children.text: The Secretary shall reserve 25 percent of the amounts made available for grants under this subsection each fiscal year to make grants for eligible projects described in subsection (b). The requirement under paragraph (6)(A)(ii) shall not apply to grants made under subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: not more than 10 percent may be used to make grants for development phase activities under paragraph (3)(B); and

children.label: ii
children.text: not more than 10 percent may be used to make grants for development phase activities under subsection (b)(5)(A)(ii)(III).

children.heading: Federal share of total project costs.—
children.label: 8 Federal share of total project costs.—

children.heading: .—Total project costs
children.label: A .—Total project costs
children.text: To be eligible for a grant under this subsection or subsection (b), an eligible applicant shall submit to the Secretary an estimate of the total costs of the project for which the grant is requested based on the best available information, including any available engineering studies, studies of economic feasibility, environmental analyses, and information on the expected use of equipment or facilities.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the Federal share of the total costs of a project under this subsection or subsection (b) shall not exceed 80 percent.

children.heading: .—Rural areas
children.label: ii .—Rural areas
children.text: The Secretary may increase the Federal share of costs above 80 percent for a project for which a grant is awarded under subsection (b) or that is located in a rural area.

children.label: A
children.text: grant funds are used for the purposes for which those funds were made available;

children.label: B
children.text: each grantee properly accounts for all expenditures of grant funds;

children.label: C
children.text: grant funds not used for such purposes and amounts not obligated or expended are returned; and

children.label: D
children.text: grant contracts are approved efficiently by the Secretary, minimizing delays for minor adjustments to project scopes and budgets, including due to inflationary effects on projects.

children.label: i
children.text: maintain such records as the Secretary considers necessary;

children.label: ii
children.text: make the records described in clause (i) available for review and audit by the Secretary; and

children.label: iii
children.text: periodically report to the Secretary such information as the Secretary considers necessary to assess progress.

children.heading: Efficient use of non-federal funds.—
children.label: B Efficient use of non-federal funds.—
I
children.text: the grant recipient may obligate and expend non-Federal funds with respect to the project for which the grant is provided; and

children.label: II
children.text: any non-Federal funds obligated or expended in accordance with subclause (I) shall be credited toward the non-Federal cost share for the project for which the grant is provided.

children.label: aa
children.text: a description of the activities the grant recipient intends to fund;

children.label: bb
children.text: a justification for advancing the activities described in item (aa), including an assessment of the effects to the project scope, schedule, and budget if the request is not approved; and

children.label: cc
children.text: the level of risk of the activities described in item (aa).

children.heading: .—Approval
children.label: II .—Approval
children.text: The Secretary shall approve or disapprove each request submitted under subclause (I).

children.heading: .—Compliance with applicable requirements
children.label: III .—Compliance with applicable requirements
children.text: Any obligation or expenditure of non-Federal funds under clause (i) shall be in compliance with all applicable requirements, including any requirements included in the grant agreement.

children.label: I
children.text: affect the signing of a grant agreement or other applicable grant procedures with respect to the applicable grant;

children.label: II
children.text: create an obligation on the part of the Federal Government to repay any non-Federal funds if the grant agreement is not signed; or

children.label: III
children.text: affect the ability of the recipient of the grant to obligate or expend non-Federal funds to meet the non-Federal cost share for the project for which the grant is provided after the period described in clause (i).

children.heading: .—Construction, repair, or alteration of vessels
children.label: C .—Construction, repair, or alteration of vessels
children.text: With regard to the construction, repair, or alteration of vessels, the same requirements of , United States Code, shall apply regardless of whether the location of contract performance is known when bids for such work are solicited.section 117(k) of title 23

children.heading: .—Administrative and oversight costs
children.label: A .—Administrative and oversight costs
children.text: The Secretary may retain not more than 2 percent of the amounts appropriated for each fiscal year to make grants for port development under this section for the administrative and oversight costs incurred by the Secretary to make grants for port development under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Amounts appropriated to make grants for port development under this section shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—Unexpended funds
children.label: ii .—Unexpended funds
children.text: Amounts awarded as a grant for port development under this section that are not expended by the grantee during the 5-year period following the date of the award or that are returned under paragraph (9)(C) shall remain available to the Secretary for use for grants under this subsection in a subsequent fiscal year. Any such amount may only be expended to award a grant under the same subsection of this section under which the original grant was made.

children.label: i
children.text: any port on the navigable waters of the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any harbor, marine terminal, or other shore side facility used principally for the movement of goods on inland waters (including the Great Lakes).

children.heading: .—Project
children.label: B .—Project
children.text: The term “project” includes construction, reconstruction, environmental rehabilitation, acquisition of property, including land related to the project and improvements to the land, equipment acquisition, and operational improvements.

children.heading: .—Rural area
children.label: C .—Rural area
children.text: The term “rural area” means an area that is outside an urbanized area.

children.heading: .—Resilience
children.label: D .—Resilience
children.text: The term “resilience” means the ability to anticipate, prepare for, adapt to, withstand, respond to, and recover from operational disruptions and sustain critical operations at ports, including disruptions caused by natural or manmade hazards, such as sea level rise, flooding, earthquakes, hurricanes, tsunami inundation or other extreme weather events.

children.heading: .—Strategic seaport defined
children.label: E .—Strategic seaport defined
children.text: In this subsection the term “strategic seaport” means a military port or commercial port that is subject to a port planning order or Basic Ordering Agreement (or both) that is projected to be used for the deployment of forces and shipment of ammunition or sustainment supplies in support of military operations.

children.heading: Assistance for Small Inland River and Coastal Ports and Terminals.—
children.label: b Assistance for Small Inland River and Coastal Ports and Terminals.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: From amounts reserved under subsection (a)(7)(B), the Secretary, acting through the Administrator of the Maritime Administration, shall make grants under this subsection to eligible applicants for eligible projects at a port, to and from which the average annual tonnage of cargo for the immediately preceding 3 calendar years from the time an application is submitted is less than 8,000,000 short tons, as determined using United States Army Corps of Engineers data or data provided by an independent audit.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If an eligible applicant provides data by an independent audit for purposes of paragraph (1), the Secretary shall use such data to make a tonnage determination if the Secretary determines that it is acceptable to use such data instead of using Corps of Engineers data.

children.heading: .—Acceptable use of data
children.label: B .—Acceptable use of data
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), an acceptable use of data means that the Secretary has determined such data is a reasonable substitute for Army Corps data.

children.heading: .—Justification
children.label: C .—Justification
children.text: If the Secretary makes a determination pursuant to subparagraph (A) that it is not acceptable to use independent audit data provided by an eligible applicant, the Secretary shall provide the eligible applicant with notification of, and justification for, such determination.

children.heading: .—Tonnage determination
children.label: 3 .—Tonnage determination
children.text: In making a determination of the average annual tonnage of cargo using Corps of Engineers data for purposes of evaluating an application of an eligible applicant pursuant to paragraph (1), the Secretary shall use data that is specific to the eligible applicant.

children.label: i
children.text: the economic advantage and the contribution to freight transportation at a port; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the competitive disadvantage of such a port;

children.label: B
children.text: not make more than 1 award per applicant under this subsection for each fiscal year appropriation; and

children.label: C
children.text: take into consideration the degree to which a project would promote the enhancement and efficiencies of a port.

children.label: I
children.text: within the boundary of a port; or

children.label: II
children.text: outside the boundary of a port, but is directly related to port operations or to an intermodal connection to a port; and

children.label: I
children.text: making capital improvements, including to piers, wharves, docks, terminals, and similar structures used principally for the movement of goods;

children.label: II
children.text: acquiring, improving, repairing, or maintaining transportation or physical infrastructure, buildings, or equipment;

children.label: III
children.text: performing development phase activities described in subsection (a)(3)(B) related to carrying out an activity described in this clause; and

children.label: IV
children.text: otherwise fulfilling the purposes for which such assistance is provided.

children.label: i
children.text: direct ownership of either a facility or equipment to be procured using funds awarded under this subsection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that equipment procured using such funds be new.

children.label: A
children.text: projects conducted on property outside the boundary of a port unless such property is directly related to port operations or to an intermodal connection to a port;

children.label: B
children.text: any single grant award more than 10 percent of total allocation of funds to carry out this subsection per fiscal year appropriation; or

children.label: C
children.text: activities, including channel improvements or harbor deepening that is part of a Federal channel or an access channel associated with a Federal channel, authorized, as of the date of the application for assistance under this subsection, to be carried out by of the United States Army Corps of Engineers.

children.label: i
children.text: be incurred prior to the date on which assistance is provided; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include a loan agreement, a commitment from investors, cash on balance sheet, or other contributions determined acceptable by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Determination of effectiveness
children.label: B .—Determination of effectiveness
children.text: In determining whether a project meets the criteria under clauses (i), (iii), (iv), (v), and (vi) of subsection (a)(6)(A), the Secretary shall accept documentation used to obtain a commitment of the matching funds covered by this paragraph, including feasibility studies, business plans, investor prospectuses, loan applications, or similar documentation.

coordinate with other Federal agencies to expedite the process established under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) for the improvement of port facilities to improve the efficiency of the transportation system, to increase port security, or to provide greater access to port facilities;42 U.S.C. 4321
seek to coordinate all reviews or requirements with appropriate Federal, State, and local agencies; and
in addition to any financial assistance provided under subsection (a) or subsection (b), provide such technical assistance to any eligible applicants as described in subsection (a)(2).
